_notes: Custom dialogue script for Specialist Kovacs at LC-4 Relay.
mood_change: positive = improves mood, negative = sours mood, 0 = no change.
response_pool: Kovacs picks one randomly. Use {name} for farm name placeholder.
unlocks: list of topic IDs this response makes available. Strict limit of ONE id per object to match singular engine injection.
tier: 1 = always available, 2 = unlocked by a tier 1 topic baseline or progressive tier 2 chains.
visible_label: what the player sees as the button text.
mood_hint: optional subtle hint shown under Kovacs' reply every time this topic is picked.

topics.visible_label: How are you doing, Kovacs?
topics.mood_hint: He appreciates basic professionalism.
response_pool: Surviving. Just another shift in the tin can.
Managing. Data traffic's heavy today.
Could be worse. I could be stationed on an unpressurized cargo rock.
Still here. The hull hasn't breached yet.

topics.visible_label: Anything good in the cafeteria today?
topics.mood_hint: He is highly particular about his daily routines.
response_pool: Terrible. They ran out of the good coffee beans again. It's synthetic sludge.
Don't ask. If I drink one more cup of this dehydrated chicory blend, I'm filing a protest.
The automated food dispensers are acting up. Can't even get a clean meal ration.
They subbed the morning protein blocks with generic brand paste. Dark day.
Nutritious but completely devoid of joy. Everything tastes like chalk today.
Standard ration blocks. Exactly the same garbage as yesterday.

topics.visible_label: How did a guy with your crisp uniform end up routing manifests?
topics.mood_hint: This clearly touches a heavy nerve about his career.
response_pool: Seniority bottlenecks. Aced the command track, yet here I sit sorting logistics.
The board claims it's operational necessity. They don't know how to read an exam profile.
Assignment committee doesn't look at merit. I'm just overqualified.
Station politics, frankly. I'm meant for a bridge, not a switchboard.
Every appeal gets buried in bureaucracy. Someone with my training should run a crew.
Let's just say my talents are currently frozen in sub-optimal infrastructure.

topics.visible_label: You look exceptionally well-prepared for a standard shift.
topics.mood_hint: He prides himself on maintaining officer standards.
response_pool: Thanks. Discipline is my only defense against deep-space rot. Even in my small quarters.
Appreciate it. An officer represents the fleet regardless of his current placement.
Standards don't drop just because you're in a tin can. It keeps me sane.
Someone's got to maintain standards. The logistics crew down on deck three look disgusting.
If you let your routine slip, the isolation wins. Barnaby hates when I look sloppy.
Press this fabric every morning. My quarters are spotless, unlike the cargo deck.

topics.visible_label: Have the communication lines been quiet today?
topics.mood_hint: He loves monitoring and judging the chatter of other sectors.
response_pool: Quiet? No. Channels are flooded with absolute amateurs messing up basic data protocols.
Spent half my shift logging standard protocol infractions from greenhorn farmers.
Every honyocker in the quadrant is hitting their transponder for minor soil issues.
Sub-space bands are chaotic today. Nobody understands audio etiquette.
Just a stream of surface operators failing to balance their atmospheric grids.
Exhausting. Way too much chatter from people who shouldn't have licenses.

topics.visible_label: Is the background rumble on the station always this loud?
topics.mood_hint: He hates the physical reality of his desk location.
response_pool: The primary ventilation scrubber's right behind my partition. Constant noise.
If I had my own command bridge, acoustic dampening would be standard. This is a joke.
Environmental controls refuse to damp the vibrations. My teeth rattle during peak hours.
Deck plating vibrates whenever a heavy freighter docks. Drives me mad.

topics.visible_label: Is the artificial gravity working normally today?
topics.mood_hint: He spends a lot of time monitoring basic station metrics.
response_pool: Stable enough. Though I feel a slight micro-gravity drift in the mornings.
Engineers were messing with the magnetic dampeners earlier. Left a glitch in the core.
Holding steady at zero-point-nine-eight gees. Fluctuation's standard when capacitors cycle.
Gravity grid's clear. No major system drift logged on my display.

topics.visible_label: Are there any interesting supply ships docking today?
topics.mood_hint: He closely tracks all transit lanes.
response_pool: Nothing but heavy ore freighters and bulk haulers. Entirely tedious.
Just standard logistics transports. They're dropping mineral crates on deck two.
Arrival boards are full of corporate haulage. No exploration vessels, unfortunately.

topics.visible_label: Let us skip the pleasantries and open the trade lines.
topics.mood_hint: He detests being treated like a machine.
response_pool: Right. Straight to business then. Fine by me.
Manifest's open. Let's speed this up.
Always a pleasure. Processing data now.
Skipping updates. State your network payload request.

topics.visible_label: Have the ventilation registers been dusted recently?
topics.mood_hint: He has no strong feelings about standard maintenance.
response_pool: Scheduled for every second Tuesday. Nothing exciting.
Maintenance handles that. I don't track dust levels.
Sterile environment's standard. Clean as they need to be.

topics.visible_label: Is your terminal screen brightness comfortable?
topics.mood_hint: Display preferences do not affect his operational status.
response_pool: At seventy percent. Standard office regulation for twelve-hour watches.
Auto-dimmer's functional. No complaints about terminal illumination.
Contrast's fine. Standard amber text on black layout.

topics.visible_label: Does the station run on fleet standard time?
topics.mood_hint: Chronological alignment is a standard background metric.
response_pool: Coordinated Universal Time. Aligns with primary colony nodes.
Synchronized with base command. No adjustments required.
Standard fleet rotation. Twenty-four hours, though we lack a sunset.

topics.visible_label: Is your inventory of office supplies sufficient?
topics.mood_hint: Station logistics guarantees basic desk operational limits.
response_pool: Got half a carton of replacement stylus caps. Lasts the quarter.
Requisitions are approved quarterly. Supply levels are adequate.
Terminal stylus works. Don't use physical paper, so limits are negligible.

topics.visible_label: What is your preferred terminal backup schedule?
topics.mood_hint: Backups are automatically processed by backend protocols.
response_pool: Automated system runs every six hours. Manual operations are redundant.
Cloud storage handles everything. No input required on my end.
Local drive copies files on the hour. Completely standard routine.

topics.visible_label: Are you worried about the upcoming quarterly corporate audit?
topics.mood_hint: He detests arbitrary administrative inspections.
response_pool: Worried? No. Annoyed? Extremely. They'll spend days checking cable ties.
Audit prep's just endless paperwork. Waste of network bandwidth.
Auditors are spreadsheet clerks. They've never seen a real grid error.

topics.visible_label: How do you get any exercise in a cramped relay station like this?
topics.mood_hint: He hates the physical limitations of his posting.
response_pool: Use a tension band twice a day. Uninspired, but prevents atrophy.
Small treadmill on deck three's got shot bearings. Sounds like a rock crusher.
Pace the command deck. Twelve paces forward, twelve back. Riveting.

topics.visible_label: Is your twelve-hour monitor shift standard for your rank?
topics.mood_hint: He feels overworked and underappreciated.
response_pool: Standard for this posting. High-tier stations get proper relief officers.
Yes. Though twelve hours becomes fourteen when the relief operator oversleeps.
Twelve hours staring at blinking cargo codes. Retinas are fried.

topics.visible_label: Is the hazard pay worth being stuck in deep space?
topics.mood_hint: He considers financial prying highly unprofessional.
response_pool: Highly personal data. Discuss cargo logistics, not my bank scrip.
Compensation's regulated by rank. It's... undervalued. Leave it at that.
Hazard pay? Safe relay station means zero hazard bonus. Just basic scrip.

topics.visible_label: Is there any way you can speed up your manifest processing times?
topics.mood_hint: He hates having his absolute competence questioned.
response_pool: Process at maximum safe speed. Want haste? Expect regulatory errors.
Processing times are top ten percent. Bottleneck's your transmitter, not me.
Haste's the enemy of accuracy. Won't compromise protocol for your rotting cargo.

topics.visible_label: How are the shipping volumes looking across the other regional sectors?
topics.mood_hint: He respects operators tracking macro logistics data.
response_pool: Sector 9's dome array is completely underperforming. Total supply bottleneck.
Regional node's backed up. Sector 14's processing facility had an encoder fail.
Standard transit congestion. Too many haulers trying to clear customs at once.
Traffic's heavy near the core lines. Mostly mineral weight manifests.
Most agricultural zones are running green today. Nothing out of pattern.
Routing queues are stable. Just standard data packets flying through.

topics.visible_label: Can you see our dome bioluminescence glow from the relay track?
topics.mood_hint: He occasionally enjoys the sparse visual landscape of his watch.
response_pool: Yeah. Clear night cycles show a faint green hue down there. It's distinct.
Caught the glow during my early diagnostic sweep. Breaks up the grey regolith.
I see it. Looks like a tiny neon dot from this altitude. Green's a nice change.
Visible on my secondary optic tracking window. Good luminosity parameters tonight.

topics.visible_label: Is the sub-space latency dragging across the planet-side array today?
topics.mood_hint: He likes narrating the technical quirks of his own equipment.
response_pool: It's a two-second bounce. Standard physics, operator. Can't rewrite reality.
Lag's standard when the gas giant passes our primary solar orbit axis.
Buffer's a bit heavy, yeah. Doesn't stop data packets unless someone screws up up here.
Always drags during peak shift handoffs. Just process your files through it.

topics.visible_label: Are the station citizens actually eating the crops I ship up?
topics.mood_hint: He knows the weight of the supply lines better than the corporate board.
response_pool: They're eating 'em. Cargo drops route straight to the block mess halls. They need it.
Your produce hits the grid within two cycles. Station's crowded, people don't ask questions.
Processed paste gets old fast. Real surface yields are a big deal down in the residential ring.
They're using it, operator. Don't expect thank-you logs from corporate, but the blocks are full.

topics.visible_label: Does anyone up on that massive orbital ring ever look down at my little dome?
topics.mood_hint: He shares the exact same operational isolation that you do.
response_pool: Honestly? No. They're just chasing scrip metrics. But I keep my eye on your telemetry.
It's five thousand citizens staring at screens, operator. They just want their rations cleared.
They don't think about the soil. Only time they care is if a transit manifest drops to zero.
You're just a coordinate on their supply ledger. Don't take it personally. I'm stuck up here too.

topics.visible_label: Are any of those other facilities managing optimal production runs?
topics.mood_hint: He prefers automated efficiency metrics over human error stories.
response_pool: Hardly. Sector 9's hydro setup's a complete mess. Filters are constantly choked.
Only Sector 3's hitting true target metrics. Their automated grid's flawlessly tuned.
They're barely scraping baseline parameters. Most surface units lack core calibration.
Don't bet on it. Half these leases won't survive the quarterly audit window.

topics.visible_label: Any improvement on the coffee front?
topics.mood_hint: He has incredibly strong feelings about his morning brew.
response_pool: None. The synth blend up here tastes like burnt rubber. I'm hunting for a real light roast.
Submitted three complaints already. Real beans are impossible to find out here.
It's all synthetic sludge. If I could get my hands on fresh ground, this station'd be bearable.
The supply vessel brought nothing but instant packets. It's a total insult to my palate.
Still drinking liquid coal. Honestly considering trading my spare boots for a bag of arabica.
No progress. Logistics manager says coffee's non-essential. Man's got no culture.

topics.visible_label: I might try growing some actual coffee beans down in my domes.
topics.mood_hint: This is the absolute way to his heart.
response_pool: Outstanding. Keep me posted on that project. Seriously.
Real beans? Do that and I'll guarantee your shipping priority.
Now that's an enterprise worth tracking. Tell me when you clear harvest.
Surface-grown roast? Manage that and I'll forgive your data errors.

topics.visible_label: Stop crying about a little bad coffee.
topics.mood_hint: He does not take kindly to people insulting his standards.
response_pool: It's about basic quality of life. Don't expect a dirt farmer to grasp it.
Not crying. Maintaining standard requirement profiles for an officer.
I'll file that comment right where it belongs. Goodbye.
Shallow perspective. Good caffeine parameters yield accurate routing maps.

topics.visible_label: What are your ambitions regarding becoming a ship captain?
topics.mood_hint: He loves boasting about his actual qualifications.
response_pool: Passed the GSC-3 Exam. Scored ninety-seventh percentile. Yet here I sit.
Filed twelve placement appeals. My command credentials speak for themselves.
Meant for a bridge, not a relay loop. Just lack political connections.
GSC-3 Level certification's no joke. Covers fleet tactics and deep survival.

topics.visible_label: I see you are not a captain yet, dork. Haha.
topics.mood_hint: He is absolutely seething.
response_pool: That does it. Processing a Complaint Form 77-A with your name on it.
Don't have to take insults from a dirt farmer. Let's process the manifest.
Watch your mouth. I can make your logistics a living nightmare down there.
Explicit protocol breach. Don't test my authority to freeze your accounts.

topics.visible_label: You are still stuck at a desk instead of a ship bridge?
topics.mood_hint: You are actively making an enemy for life.
response_pool: Highly unprofessional. Logging this as a Form 77-A compliance breach.
I'm an officer in waiting. Keep mocking me, your cargo markups'll reflect it.
Laugh all you want. When I get my bridge command, your sector's audited first.

topics.visible_label: What do you do to pass the time up there?
topics.mood_hint: He relaxes slightly when discussing his routines.
response_pool: Look after a small lunar-bonsai tree. Trimming it keeps my focus sharp.
Learning the Ionuke. Compact four-string instrument. Passes quiet hours.
Between shifts, I mostly manage Barnaby. He's a remarkably fat orange cat.
Practice manual knot-tying and navigation charts. Keeps core skills active.

topics.visible_label: Can you put Barnaby the cat on the viewscreen camera?
topics.mood_hint: He absolutely worships his cat.
response_pool: He's asleep on the core switch housing. Sends his regards.
Barnaby's busy ignoring me. He rules this entire deck deck, honestly.
He's orange, so his cognitive processing's limited. Staring at the wall now.
Washing himself on the main router frame. Won't disturb him.

topics.visible_label: How is your Ionuke practice coming along?
topics.mood_hint: He likes discussing technical progress, even musical ones.
response_pool: Slowly. Antenna interference makes the audio pickups buzz constantly.
Mastered three basic fleet cadences. Four-string layout's entirely logical.
Barnaby hates the high notes. Swats my fingers right off the fretboard.

topics.visible_label: Your hobbies sound completely ridiculous for a fleet officer.
topics.mood_hint: He will immediately withdraw into his corporate shell.
response_pool: They maintain psychological parameters during long isolation blocks. Standard protocol.
Didn't ask for your evaluation of my personal quarters. Back to business.
Barnaby's got more structural discipline than most surface operators I deal with.

topics.visible_label: Are any of the other surface farmers making mistakes?
topics.mood_hint: Nothing brings him joy quite like a catastrophic amateur failure.
response_pool: Sector 18 flooded his own domes! Absolute amateur honyocker. Hilarious.
Sector 4 vented their entire fertilizer payload into the vacuum last night.
Some honyocker in Sector 9 wiped his harvester drone firmware. Incredible.
Watching a dome failure alert in Sector 22. Forgot to latch an airlock lock.

topics.visible_label: You should have some respect for how hard farming on the moon is.
topics.mood_hint: He has zero sympathy for incompetence.
response_pool: It's basic atmospheric logic. Not rocket science. Read the manual.
If they can't balance a simple chemical chart, lose the contract.
Reserve my respect for operators who track standard data parameters.

topics.visible_label: How many Complaint Form 77-A files have you actually processed today?
topics.mood_hint: He loves using corporate policy as a weapon.
response_pool: Fourteen so far. Processing a major compliance alert for Sector 3.
Just filed a protocol non-compliance form on the morning supervisor.
Got a stack ready for regional logistics. Actions have consequences.
Love a fresh Form 77-A file. Only way to scrub these sub-space frequencies.

topics.visible_label: Keep your forms to yourself, or I will report you for bad service.
topics.mood_hint: He knows the rulebook better than you ever will.
response_pool: Go ahead. Fully compliant with Code Section 9. Terminal logs are pristine.
I'm the reporting authority for this entire hub sector. File hits my bin.
Auditing your manifest right now. I'd think twice about filing anything.

topics.visible_label: Have you ever experienced a full artificial gravity failure on the relay?
topics.mood_hint: He likes discussing the technical vulnerabilities of the hub.
response_pool: Solar flare last cycle fused dampener relays. Weightless for three hours.
Core failure on deck four last winter. Complex routing when consoles float.
Reactor had a cold restart. Wore magnetic boots just to stay anchored.

topics.visible_label: What happens to Barnaby during artificial gravity drops?
topics.mood_hint: He finds the image of his floating cat highly amusing.
response_pool: Hilarious. Floats like an orange balloon, claws out, deeply offended.
Turns into a pufferfish. Drifts around the room, glaring at me.
Have to strap him to his basket. Otherwise he drifts into the fan array.

topics.visible_label: What do you think of the cargo transport pilots docking at the hub?
topics.mood_hint: He holds commercial pilots in very low regard, and he's glad someone's asking.
response_pool: Undisciplined. Ignore basic docking tracks and drift straight into transit lanes.
Bus drivers with thrusters. Zero navigational elegance. Punch autopilot and pray.
Spend my watch yelling at freighter crews who miss their clamp alignments.

topics.visible_label: Do the pilots have any respect for the LC-4 Relay staff?
topics.mood_hint: He feels deeply underappreciated by the transit crews — venting to you helps.
response_pool: None. Treat us like automated beacons. We guide ninety percent of transit paths.
Call us desk operators. Meanwhile, we keep them from crashing into platforms.
Filed three Form 77-A compliance complaints on transport crews this watch.

hostile: LC-4 Relay, Kovacs here. State your situation.
Kovacs here. Go for LC-4. Make it quick.
Recording this channel. State your business protocol.
LC-4 Relay. No mood for data breaches today. State query.
sour: LC-4 Relay, Kovacs here. What do you need?
Kovacs here. Middle of a technical diagnostic loop.
LC-4 Relay. Standing by for trade data payload rules.
Specialist Kovacs monitoring. Present your shipping data keys.
neutral: LC-4 Relay, Kovacs here. Go ahead.
This is Kovacs, go for LC-4 Relay.
Kovacs here. Standing by for surface data sheets.
LC-4 Relay audio-active. Proceed with your transaction parameters.
happy: LC-4 Relay, Kovacs here. Good timing, actually.
Kovacs here. Core data lines are perfectly clear today.
LC-4 Relay. Caught me in a reasonable tracking window.
How's life on the surface today? Manifest queues are empty.
elated: LC-4 Relay, Kovacs here. Always good to hear from you.
Decent cycle up on the hub. What can I process for you?
Comms are optimal. Ready when you are, friend.
Barnaby's behaving and my matrix queue is empty. Let's do business.

idle_transmissions: Station Broadcast: Week {week} regional agricultural reports due. Validate your vectors.
Kovacs personal log: GSC-3 Exam certificates remain valid for three standard cycles.
LC-4 Technical Alert: Data packet loss in Sector 12. Honyockers probably tripped on cables.
Corporate Memo: Etiquette Non-Compliance Form 77-A filings up twelve percent this quarter.
Kovacs here: Barnaby's caught zero mice, but his server warming routine is flawless.
Weather Advisory: Solar radiation spike expected. Ensure dome shield logs are green.
LC-4 Relay Note: Outer shipping lanes are clear tonight. Wish I was out there charting.
Kovacs: Corrupt shipping manifest submitted. Officially processing Form 77-A code red.
Logistics Alert: Cargo backup at primary docking ring. Ensure transit crates are labeled.
Kovacs: Ask me to manually check your seed order again, I'm shutting off the receiver.
Station Chatter: Cafeteria paste machine recalibrated. Joy down another five percent.
Kovacs personal note: Lunar-bonsai cleared two centimeters new growth. Excellent density.
System Advisory: Automatic billing scripts running for Week {week}. Check Star-Scrip banks.
